description.p: UltimateSlice is a non-linear video editor for Linux. Import media, arrange clips on the timeline, and export to MP4.

screenshot.caption: UltimateSlice, Main window with project loaded

ul.li: FCPXML 1.10-1.14 import and 1.14 export with round-trip preservation of all clip properties
GTK4 application with dark theme styling
Media library import with duration probing for video, audio, and images
Source monitor with playback, scrubber, in/out marks, and timecode display
Timeline with multi-track rows, zoom/pan, clip selection, trim, move, and razor tool
Multi-track video compositing with per-clip opacity for b-roll overlays
Undo/Redo command history
Inspector panel for selected clip properties
Per-clip color correction: brightness, contrast, and saturation
Advanced color grading: shadows, midtones, and highlights sliders
Denoise and sharpness effects per clip
3D LUT support — apply .cube color look-up table files to any clip
Per-clip transform controls: scale, position offset, rotation, and horizontal/vertical flip
Per-clip crop (left, right, top, bottom) with transparent compositing
Interactive transform overlay — drag crop edges and scale handles directly in the Program Monitor
Per-clip audio volume and stereo pan controls
Per-clip speed control for fast- and slow-motion playback
Title and text overlays with font, colour, and position settings
MP4/H.264 export via ffmpeg with full color grading, LUT, crop, and speed baked in
Proxy generation for smoother editing of high-resolution media
Adaptive proxy assist: automatically switches to proxy media in regions with three or more overlapping video tracks
Occlusion-based decode skip: clips fully hidden behind an opaque overlay skip video decode entirely, reducing CPU load
Cross-dissolve transitions between clips on the timeline
Optional MCP server (--mcp) for JSON-RPC AI collaboration with 55 tools covering the full edit workflow
